findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` always passes because any object is identical to itself. This test can never fail regardless of the implementation and provides zero coverage signal.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful assertion, e.g. `assert isinstance(s, Settings)` or check a specific attribute like `assert s.environment == 'dev'`.

findings.message: Over-mocked test: `resolved_log_level` is monkeypatched away and then the mock return value is asserted. The test exercises the mock, not the real implementation — it passes even if `resolved_log_level` is completely broken.
findings.suggestion: Remove the patch and test the real behaviour, e.g. `assert Settings(environment='dev').resolved_log_level == 'DEBUG'` (already covered by `test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev`). Delete this test or cover a missing edge case without mocking the method under test.
